sourceMovie.title: The Whole Nine Yards
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit The Whole Nine Yards for the same reason it broke through in 2000: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a crime rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Jonathan Lynn keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The comedy and crime blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Gentlemen
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Whole Nine Yards clicked for you, The Gentlemen (2020) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and crime, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Whole Nine Yards?
faq.answer: Try The Gentlemen, The Sting, Analyze This—each shares DNA with The Whole Nine Yards while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Whole Nine Yards?
faq.answer: The Whole Nine Yards (2000) is best described as Comedy, Crime. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Whole Nine Yards part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "The Whole Nine/Ten Yards Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
